text:
Andy Murray芒聙聶s professional competitive career came to a close at the2024 Paris Olympics, after a storied run with his doubles partner, Daniel Evans. Representing Great Britain, the two compatriots saved match points in their final two games, keeping hope alive for one last magical run at the sport that has given Murray his greatest glory.

The two-time gold medalist, 37, was脗聽hoping to add another gold, this time in doubles. He and Evans had played inspiring tennis, coming from behind in two three-set thrillers in the round of 32 and 16. But in the quarters against Americans Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul, their luck ran out, going out in two sets, 6-2, 6-4.

Still, the Great Scot left his emotions on the clay courts of Roland Garros, inspiring tennis fans to keep rooting for the impassioned player.

It's raining in Paris 冒聼楼潞#Tennis@andy_murraypic.twitter.com/oCvPtlP5W6

芒聙聹I knew that moment was coming for the last few months,芒聙聺 Murray said after the loss in doubles,according to theNew York Post. 芒聙聹If it didn芒聙聶t happen today, that was going to be in a couple days芒聙聶 time, and I was ready for it. Obviously, I was emotional because it芒聙聶s the last time I will play a competitive match. But I am genuinely happy, just now. I芒聙聶m happy with how it芒聙聶s finished.芒聙聺

Murray is a three-time Grand Slam title holder and former No. 1 player in the world. But it's his title as two-timeOlympic gold medalistthat the Scottish pro holds sway over his great rivals Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic. Murray is the only tennis player to hold two gold medals in singles.

He won gold at the London Games in 2012. He then followed with an unprecedented repeat at the 2016 Rio Games. Only Serena and Venus Williams own more gold medals in tennis.

Murray announced that his retirement was official in the only way that he could:

Never even liked tennis anyway.

Lots of tributes poured in, of course, including his great rival Federer, who he beat at the 2012 London Games to avenge his loss to the Swiss at Wimbledon the month before. Both matches were played at the SW19.

Roger with nothing but respect and admiration for#SirAndy冒聼聮聶@rogerfederer|@andy_murraypic.twitter.com/VEYsHv4RmS

Nadal芒聙聰who is also possiblycontemplating his own retirement芒聙聰paid tribute to Murray.

All the memories, all the moments shared... 冒聼楼潞@RafaelNadalreminisces on his time with@andy_murray冒聼聶聦#SirAndypic.twitter.com/kngf2xyUFa

Djokovic芒聙聰who just made the Olympics final for the first time in his career芒聙聰also sent his support.

A legend honours a legend 冒聼聭聫@DjokerNolehas a special message for@andy_murray芒聺陇茂赂聫#SirAndypic.twitter.com/Z7cZrDAGhH

Murray followed up his mic drop tweet with a heartfelt thank you to his fans.

冒聼聯搂冒聼聯虏 A message to you from@andy_murray冒聼聨楼@TeamGBpic.twitter.com/LXrBskUaKI

Murray won his first Grand Slam title against Djokovic at the 2012U.S. Openin a five-set thriller. In 2013, the Glasgow native became the first British man to win Wimbledon in almost 80 years. It was no small task, having to face down Djokovic again in the final, which he won in three sets. He repeated the feat three years later, in 2016, again in three, this time against Canada芒聙聶s Milos Raonic.

But it was 2016 that was a remarkable year for the three-time major winner. Murray reached his tennis zenith with the world No. 1 ranking, a Masters 1,000 title, Olympic gold in singles, a second Wimbledon title and the ATP Finals championship. No other tennis player has realized all of that in one year.

But the 2016 achievements also took a toll on Murray芒聙聶s physicality, beginning a streak of injuries that he never really recovered from.

It's possible that Murray suffered the most during the Golden Era of Tennis, when he often had to go up against some of the best players the sport has ever seen: Federer, Nadal and Djokovic (to be a true list you would have to include Rod Laver, in my opinion). It is a testament to Murray芒聙聶s drive that he accomplished so much during that time. His iconic back-to-back golds in singles puts him in a category all his own.

Wimbledon posted a tribute to the Scotsman, who represented so much pride for the U.K. They had held a celebratory ceremony for Murray at this year芒聙聶s tournament.

A story we芒聙聶ll never tire of reliving.Andy Murray芒聙聶s#Wimbledonjourney - in his own words 芒聹篓pic.twitter.com/WjKZvoBfVo

A very warm welcome for a very special person. 芒聺陇茂赂聫Andy Murray received a guard of honour from Team GB as he arrived back.  冒聼陇聺#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/iBUA5PDRH0

It was a privilege to share the court with you, Andy! 冒聼楼鹿冒聼聶聫冒聼聫禄 Congratulations on a legendary career and for being an example to all. You will always have a fan here! 芒聺陇茂赂聫@andy_murray冒聼聯赂 Gettypic.twitter.com/QCNRERPMIw

Congratulations@andy_murrayon an amazing career! You gave it everything, and never left a stone unturned. Enjoy your retirement and look forward to a game of golf soon! 冒聼聨戮 冒聼聡卢冒聼聡搂pic.twitter.com/vybgONpBhe

An emotional end to an incredible career 冒聼楼鹿Always an inspiration, Laureus Award winner@Andy_Murrayretires as true legend of the sport.#Olympics|#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/aSBrbHKkUd

What芒聙聶s next for the philanthropic Sir Andy Murray?According to his wife, she says she芒聙聶ll be happy to have him home, since she 芒聙聹needs help with the children.芒聙聺 They have four.

The All England Lawn and Tennis Club will also dedicate the Centre Court in Sir Andy芒聙聶s honor.

The LTA has announced that The Queen芒聙聶s Club Centre Court is going to be renamed Andy Murray Arena.Incredible 芒聺陇茂赂聫茂赂聫pic.twitter.com/V7tbUJZ4Hv

title: Andy Murray says goodbye to tennis at the 2024 Paris Olympics
description: Andy Murray officially retired following the Olympic Games in Paris. The All England Tennis and Lawn Club announced they will name the Queen芒聙聶s Club court after the Scotsman.
